_about: Mock numbers for the 'Scalability story' page (web-demo/scalability.html). All values are synthetic and editable in one place: changing a number here rewrites every tile and re-shapes the chart on the page automatically. See web-demo/scalability.js for derived-value formulas. Schema v3: drop the cloud-cost comparator framing — the page is purely about compute (density, throughput, daily volume, tokens) and intentionally does not compare against a frontier-API $/task rate. Sizing now models the multi-socket-tray (4 sockets / tray, 32 trays / rack) variants of each SKU rather than the older dual-socket 1U baseline.

instances.label: Intel Xeon CWF
instances.subtitle: 1 tray · 1152 vCPU · 12 TB
instances.notes: Quad-socket tray reference: 288 E-cores per socket · 4 sockets per tray. ~10 GB / thread, DDR5 generation tuned for inference bandwidth — agent pods fit comfortably on a single tray.

instances.label: Intel GNR
instances.subtitle: 1 tray · 1024 vCPU · 12 TB
instances.notes: Quad-socket tray reference: 128 P-cores (256 SMT threads) per socket · 4 sockets per tray. ~12 GB / thread. AMX accelerates the SLM / analytics offload path.

instances.label: Intel Xeon CWF rack
instances.subtitle: 32 trays · 36,864 vCPU · 384 TB
instances.notes: 42U rack populated with 32 quad-socket CWF trays (remaining U is leaf switches and PDUs). Per-tray sizing identical to the single-tray row. Linear scale-out assumes the front-door router fans agent sessions across trays; cross-tray coordination cost is folded into the queueing curve.

instances.label: Intel GNR rack
instances.subtitle: 32 trays · 32,768 vCPU · 384 TB
instances.notes: 42U rack populated with 32 quad-socket GNR trays. AMX accelerates per-tray SLM throughput; vLLM is sharded by tray, not across trays. Same per-tray curve as the single-tray GNR row, fanned across 32 trays by the LiteLLM/router layer.

instances.label: Mixed rack — agent half (CWF)
instances.subtitle: 16× CWF agent half · 16× GNR offload half

composition.model: Intel Xeon CWF (4-socket tray) — agent host

instances.notes: Mixed-SKU rack: CWF half hosts agent session pods (high core count, ample memory); GNR half hosts vLLM and the analytics offload workers (AMX-accelerated). The density tile reflects only the CWF agent half — the 16 GNR trays do not host agents, they absorb the offloaded inference and analytics load. Total rack capacity (CWF + GNR) is shown by the rack-capacity tile.

workloads.label: Terminal agent (small)
workloads.subtitle: engineering / shell tasks
workloads.notes: One agent ≈ 1 vCPU + 2 GB resident; per-task wall-clock 8s end-to-end including a single LLM round-trip. Human equivalent: a junior engineer running the same shell task by hand.

workloads.label: Market research (medium)
workloads.subtitle: multi-step research + offload
workloads.notes: Research agent that does retrieval, multi-call summarization, and writes a short report. 4 vCPU and 45s wall-clock are typical when offload keeps the heavy step off the agent pod.

scenarios.label: Xeon CWF · 1 tray · small agents
scenarios.story: How dense a single quad-socket CWF tray gets when packed with light terminal agents.

scenarios.label: GNR · 1 tray · research workload
scenarios.story: Heavier research agents on a GNR tray with multi-call inference and offload — same density story, different per-task profile.

scenarios.label: Xeon CWF rack · 32 trays · small agents
scenarios.story: Linear scale-out of the per-tray story. A full 42U rack of quad-socket CWF trays packs ~36k concurrent terminal agents and shifts the daily volume / tokens tiles by the same tray multiplier.

scenarios.label: GNR rack · 32 trays · research workload
scenarios.story: Same research workload as the single-tray row, fanned across a full GNR rack. Each tray serves its own vLLM + offload stack; LiteLLM / the front-door router handles routing.

scenarios.label: Mixed rack · 16× CWF agent + 16× GNR offload
scenarios.story: Half of the rack runs agent session pods on CWF (high core count); the other half runs the SLM and analytics offload on GNR (AMX-accelerated). The density numbers below are for the CWF agent half — see the rack-capacity tile for the full 32-tray footprint.

rack_builder._about: Interactive rack composer. Each node_type carries a baseline_scenario_id pointing at one of the per-tray scenarios above; the builder reads sweet-spot concurrency / throughput from that baseline and multiplies by tray count to project rack-scale numbers live as the user adds or removes trays. Custom mode also synthesizes a latency curve from the same baseline by scaling concurrency / throughput by tray count and holding p50 / p95 constant — same assumption that drives the preset rack curves. Keeping the baseline in scenarios[] (instead of duplicating numbers here) means edits to a single-tray curve automatically reshape the builder.
rack_builder.default_total_summary: 16× CWF + 16× GNR — the shipped 'mixed rack' baseline.

node_types.label: Intel Xeon CWF
node_types.subtitle: agent host (1 tray)
node_types.notes: Hosts agent session pods. Scales out by 1 vCPU / 2 GB per agent.

node_types.label: Intel GNR
node_types.subtitle: SLM + offload (1 tray)
node_types.notes: Hosts vLLM and the research / analytics offload workers. AMX-accelerated.
